world.title: U.S. and Iran mull second round of talks as Hormuz blockade begins
world.description: The news comes as the U.S. pressed ahead with a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz as a way of ramping up pressure on the regime in Tehran.

world.title: Trump administration agrees to keep Pride flag at Stonewall monument
world.description: A lawsuit was filed after the flag was removed this year from the Stonewall National Monument, which commemorates a milestone for the gay rights movement.

world.title: Trump administration agrees to keep Pride flag at Stonewall monument
world.description: A lawsuit was filed after the flag was removed this year from the Stonewall National Monument, which commemorates a milestone for the gay rights movement.

world.title: Embattled GOP Rep. Tony Gonzales announces he’s stepping down from Congress
world.description: Embattled Republican Rep. Tony Gonzales announced Monday that he would step down from Congress, just days before he faced the possibility of a high-stakes vote that could have made him the seventh member to ever be expelled from the House.

world.title: Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual misconduct claims
world.description: Four women have accused the congressman and ex-candidate for California governor of misconduct ranging from sexual harassment to rape, which he has denied.

world.title: South Africa's mining industry faces money laundering problems
world.description: It is a move which could affect new mining opportunities for investors: On Oct. 18, 2025, South Africa was officially removed from […]

world.title: Orbán’s defeat may signal shift in Europe’s far-right, illiberal tide says Michael Ignatieff
world.description: For Michael Ignatieff, news that Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule in Hungary had come to an end was reason enough to raise a glass. He speaks about what Orbán’s defeat and Péter Magyar's victory could mean for geopolitics.

world.title: US's 20-year uranium freeze demand vs Iran's 5: Deadlock that derailed truce talks
world.description: A disagreement over Iran’s nuclear programme emerged as the central sticking point in weekend talks between Iran and the United States in Pakistan, with Washington seeking a 20-year freeze on uranium enrichment while Tehran countered with a five-year limit.

business.title: Influential NZ fashion label Wynn Hamlyn is 'pressing pause'
business.description: After ten years of distinct New Zealand craftsmanship, Wynn Hamlyn's current collection will be its last - for now.

business.title: STG Buys Carrier Logistics to Launch Autonomous Freight Agents
business.description: Private equity firm STG plans to develop an AI-native less-than-truckload solution with its acquisition of Carrier Logistics.

business.title: Oil prices drop as US, Iran weigh more talks with blockade in place
business.description: Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, fell 1.55 per cent to US$97.82 a barrel as at 9.40am Singapore time. Read more at straitstimes.com.

business.title: Introducing The Panerai Luminor 31 Giorni PAM01631
business.description: A month-long power reserve comes to the Luminor for the first time in a new 200-piece limited edition.

business.title: Stocks in Asia Poised to Climb on Iran Deal Hopes: Markets Wrap
business.description: (Bloomberg) -- Stocks in Asia were set to advance, tracking Wall Street gains as US President Donald Trump raised hopes for an Iran deal even as a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz took effect.

business.title: What Auckland is doing wrong when it comes to recycling - and how it can change
business.description: Explainer - A third of the city's recycling ends up in a landfill. What are we doing wrong, and how can we fix it?

business.title: Iran war: IMF, World Bank to support countries as surging fertiliser prices ignite food security fears
business.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) and World Bank Group (WBG) have pledged to support member countries affected by the Middle East conflict, as surging oil, gas, and fertiliser prices ignite fears of food security and job losses.

business.title: Introducing The Chopard L.U.C. 1860 With An “Areuse Blue” Gold Dial
business.description: Three years after the launch of the great salmon-dialed LUC 1860, that model is being replaced with a beautiful blue variation.

science.title: How Jupiter Cultivated More Large Moons Than Saturn
science.description: The two largest planets in our Solar System, Jupiter and Saturn, also have the largest satellite systems, or the most

science.title: The Most Quiet Place We've Ever Listened From!
science.description: For the first time in history, scientists have used a spacecraft on the far side of the Moon to search for signals from extraterrestrial intelligence. China's Chang'E-4 lander sat in the most radio quiet location humanity has ever placed an instrument, shielded from Earth's constant electronic chatter by the entire bulk of the Moon itself. They found nothing but that is almost beside the point!

science.title: ‘Cosmic volcano’ erupts after 100 million years of silence
science.description: A giant “cosmic volcano” has erupted in deep space as a colossal black hole reawakens after 100 million years | Newsreel

science.title: X-ray Beam Communication Enables Advanced Interstellar Signaling
science.description: Research on X-ray and gamma-ray beams highlights their advantages in interstellar communication, offering new strategies for detecting extraterrestrial signals.

science.title: Tiny Particles In Arctic Ponds May Play Role In Cloud Formation, Climate Change
science.description: Tiny particles bubbling up from the tops of melting sea ice into the Arctic sky may be a key, understudied

science.title: Two Monsters, One Galaxy, and a Collision 100 Years Away!
science.description: Deep in the heart of a distant galaxy, two monsters are locked in a death spiral and for the first time, they have been caught them in the act. A new study has confirmed the first close pair of supermassive black holes ever detected, orbiting each other every 121 days and closing in fast. If the models are right, they could collide within a century.

science.title: New chip traps light for millions of cycles using novel method
science.description: New photonic chip traps light for millions of cycles using protected atomically thin materials.

science.title: When giant animals vanished, early humans reinvented their tools
science.description: A new study shows that early humans shifted from hunting giants to smaller animals, shaping tools, survival, and intelligence.

ai.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Triggers Cybersecurity Race
ai.description: ARK Invest says AI models such as Mythos are a tailwind for cybersecurity firms CrowdStrike, Cloudflare and Rubrik.

ai.title: Man accused in Molotov cocktail attack on OpenAI CEO’s home charged with attempted murder
ai.description: The man accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s home in San Francisco was opposed to AI, writing about its purported risk to humanity and “our impending extinction,” according to court documents.

ai.title: Man accused in Molotov cocktail attack of OpenAI CEO's home charged with attempted murder
ai.description: San Francisco District Attorney Brooke Jenkins says that 20-year-old Daniel Moreno-Gama has been charged with attempting to kill the OpenAI CEO as well as a security guard at the residence when he threw the incendiary device at the gate of Sam Altman’s home on April 10. Moreno-Gama was arrested a short time later outside the headquarters of OpenAI, and police said that he threatened to burn down the business. Court documents say the man was opposed to artificial intelligence and had a list of other AI tech executives.

ai.title: Man accused in Molotov cocktail attack of OpenAI CEO’s home charged with attempted murder

ai.title: Dash Social Brings Canva Into Its Platform, Connecting Design, Data, and Publishing for Social Teams
ai.description: NEW YORK, April 13, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Dash Social today announced a new integration that brings Canva into its platform, combining creative excellence with creative intelligence. Canva, the all-in-one design tool used by hundreds of millions of people worldwide, can now be accessed from Dash to create or edit content and return with a finished asset ready to schedule or publish. With Dash Social’s Vision AI built in, teams can understand how their content is predicted to perform and make informed edits before going live.

ai.title: Anthropic’s AI model sparks rush from industry, government to batten down defence hatches
ai.description: Financial and tech sectors and national governments brace for security threat posed by latest iteration of Mythos

ai.title: Meet the man accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman: a 20-year-old AI doomer
ai.description: Officials found a document discussing AI’s purported risk to humanity and “our impending extinction,” according to the criminal complaint.
